title: Where can I find my boards curl code?
excerpt:
Hi all, I cant seem to locate where I would find my link to each field type (short text, drop down) etc. 
I am trying to locate the curl code, so I can GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE. This is for an API integration I want to c…

posts.author: hgerken14 (Hunter Gerken)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all, I cant seem to locate where I would find my link to each field type (short text, drop down) etc.
I am trying to locate the curl code, so I can GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE. This is for an API integration I want to create with my existing board.

Thank you!

posts.author: TMNXT-Dev (Ilja Zweigert)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @hgerken14 ,

first of all, welcome to the community.

There are a ton of resources in the community that can help you. I picked some specific for you.
Check this thread out:

cURL Examples for API v2 Announcements
Ever wonder how to send requests to our API using cURL? Here you go A few notes: Executing cURL from the terminal removes backslashes and passes the escaped characters in the resulting string. As a result, if you want to pass \" to the server you need to include \\\". I ran these examples from the command line on my MacOS machine. Some Windows terminals don’t like single quotes, so you may need to replace them with " if you’re running these examples on a windows machine. If…

If you look for the how and where, look at this awesome summary:

READ THIS FIRST - Useful threads and development resources Announcements
Follow this section! It’s a place for the monday dev success team to put feature announcements, helpful examples, and tips. Here is a list of threads that might be helpful for folks designing and building monday apps. This document is a work in progress, and we’ll keep adding to it as y’all share your tips and tricks. Getting started with the basics Here’s some basic documentation to get you started building with the monday apps framework. Intro to monday apps Quickstart guides Code example…

Happy hacking

Greetings

posts.author: Helen (Helen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @hgerken14 welcome!

Yes, I would definitely recommend starting out by checking out the resources @TMNXT-Dev linked as you get started.

Let us know if you have any other specific questions-- happy to take a look and chime in to help!

posts.author: AlexSavchuk (Alex Savchuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Heyy @hgerken14

My warmest welcome to the community as well! I wanted to chime in with a little bit of detail here and mention that our API only supports POST requests, even if you are only querying data. Using Get may be possible in some cases, but is not a best practice, and we strongly recommend using POST as your method when sending API calls to monday.com

Happy building! I hope you were able to find some helpful resources on the community.

-Alex

posts.author: system (system)
posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.
